page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-16-lssd is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, providing dedicated CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 128 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Local storage is bundled via a 402 GB NVMe SSD, while no GPU accelerators are present on this instance type.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core score in the top 25% and high marks in Geekbench text processing. Multi-core performance, software compilation, and database operations fall within the average range. However, memory write bandwidth for large uncached blocks is relatively poor, landing in the bottom 10%, which represents a notable tradeoff for write-heavy memory operations. Small-model LLM text generation performs strongly, whereas large-model prompt processing is limited.

Given its high memory density and local NVMe SSD storage, this server type offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive GPU acceleration. The combination of dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 128 GB of RAM makes it highly suitable for database hosting, in-memory caching, text processing pipelines, and medium-scale web serving.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-16-lssd is a memory-optimized server featuring 16 vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor on the x86_64 architecture. It includes 8 physical cores with dedicated CPU allocation and is bundled with a 402 GB local NVMe SSD, though it lacks GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU execution and text processing capabilities, alongside average multi-core and database performance. A key tradeoff is its poor large-block memory write bandwidth, which sits in the bottom 10%. This configuration provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy workloads that benefit from local high-speed storage. It is well-suited for database operations, in-memory caching, text processing, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores, high memory density, and local NVMe SSD storage for database workloads.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 128 GB RAM powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 CPU
Includes a local 402 GB NVMe SSD for fast temporary storage
Strong single-core CPU and text processing performance
Tradeoff of lower large-block memory write bandwidth
Optimized for database hosting, caching, and memory-heavy workloads
